caption: Group Management
description: Group Management events report lifecycle management of a group, as well as privileges, roles, and resources associated with the group. There are two ways updates to the state of a group may be expressed: Using the Update activity, for example when updating multiple items at one time, the updated_group reflects the changes made to group. Using discrete activities, such as Assign Privileges the state change is expressed by the privileges attribute. Optionally, on success, the updated_group reflects the new state of the group.
name: group_management

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the activity that triggered the event. The target of each activity is the group attribute.

1.caption: Assign Privileges
1.description: Assign privileges to the group. Use privileges as privileges to be assigned.

2.caption: Revoke Privileges
2.description: Revoke privileges from the group. Use privileges as privileges to be revoked.

3.caption: Add User
3.description: Add user to the group. Use user as the user to be added.

4.caption: Remove User
4.description: Remove user from the group. Use user as the user to be removed.

6.caption: Create
6.description: Create a group. Populate the group object with details of the group to be created.

7.caption: Add Subgroup
7.description: Add a subgroup to the group. Use sub_group as the subgroup to be added.

8.caption: Remove Subgroup
8.description: Remove a subgroup from the group. Use sub_group as the subgroup to be removed.

9.caption: Update
9.description: Update the group. The updated group details should be populated in the updated_group attribute.

10.caption: Attach Policies
10.description: Attach one or more IAM Policies to the group. Use policies as policies to be attached.

11.caption: Detach Policies
11.description: Detach one or more IAM Policies from the group. Use policies as policies to be detached.

12.caption: Assign Roles
12.description: Assign one or more roles to the group. Use iam_roles for the specific roles to be assigned.

13.caption: Remove Roles
13.description: Remove one or more roles from the group. Use iam_roles for the specific roles to be removed.

14.caption: Add Users
14.description: Add one or more users to the group. Use users as the users to be added.

15.caption: Remove Users
15.description: Remove one or more users from the group. Use users as the users to be removed.

group.description: Group that was the target of the event.

iam_roles.description: One or more roles assigned to or removed from a group.

policies.description: Details about the IAM policies associated with the Attach/Detach Policy activities.

privileges.description: A list of privileges assigned to the group.

resource.description: Resource that the privileges give access to.

resources.description: Resources that the privileges, policies, and roles give access to.

subgroup.description: A subgroup added to or removed from group.

updated_group.description: The intended state of the group after the update. On Success, represents the actual post-update state.

user.description: A user that was added to or removed from group.

users.description: A set of users that were added to or removed from group.
